title: Concrete preview / advanced-preview release targets for all buildable products
revised_by: RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-ALL-BUILDABLE-001; RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-WRAPPERS-001; RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-BUILD-ROWS-001; RELEASE-PREVIEW-UNBLOCK-ALL-BUNDLES-001; RELEASE-PREVIEW-FAN-WEBFLASH-ELIGIBILITY-001; STABLE-PROMOTION-RECONCILE-001 (Bedroom promoted to stable v1.0.5 on 2026-06-08 and Kitchen to stable v1.0.6 on 2026-06-09, both under owner risk-acceptance waivers; their targets here moved preview -> stable / published-stable and the stable baseline version moved to the live v1.0.4)

fan_webflash_eligibility_decision.rule: FanRelay / FanPWM / FanDAC are now preview / manual-preview WebFlash-import ELIGIBLE: each carries webflash_import_eligibility.eligible=true with exposure_class=acknowledgement-gated, so downstream WebFlash no longer treats the catalog webflash_build_matrix=false or the prior WEBFLASH-RELAY-001/PWM/DAC 'blocked' framing as a preview-import blocker. Eligibility authorises an Advanced-install-only preview import only; it does NOT promote anything to stable / full release and does NOT add a committed config/webflash-builds.json row. The fan-token guardrail (scripts/list_release_targets.py) and catalog webflash_build_matrix=false stand, so fans stay on the manual-preview lane and the actual WebFlash-side one-click committed import is the separately queued downstream WF-IMPORT-RELAY-001 / WF-IMPORT-PWM-001 / WF-IMPORT-DAC-001 follow-up. FanTRIAC stays excluded (eligible=false, advanced-manual-preview, HW-005 build blocker) and is handled by its own separate TRIAC-specific PR.

unblock_all_bundles_decision.rule: Hardware-proof / bench-evidence / compliance / commercial-availability blockers gate STABLE / full release ONLY and never block a preview artifact for a buildable target. Each target below carries explicit preview_allowed / preview_warning_required / blocker_is_stable_only flags so the stable-only nature of every recorded stable_blocker is machine-checkable. The only thing that can stop an actual preview cut is a genuine buildability blocker (recorded as build_blocker; today only FanTRIAC carries one, under HW-005). No target is promoted to stable / recommended / default / buyable; Simple install stays the stable Bathroom PoE build only; the advanced-manual-preview lane is the only exposure path for the risky / unverified TRIAC build.

revision_note: RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-ALL-BUILDABLE-001 aligns the concrete targets with the RELEASE-PREVIEW-ALL-PRODUCTS-001 policy decision: EVERY buildable product is a preview (or advanced-preview) RELEASE target. Lack of hardware proof blocks STABLE only and never blocks a preview artifact. FanRelay / FanPWM / FanDAC move from passive 'manual-candidate-only / not releasable' to PREVIEW release targets delivered via the 'manual-preview' lane; FanTRIAC moves from the 'blocked' lane to the 'advanced-manual-preview' lane (no longer blocked-from-preview; only the HW-005 buildability blocker prevents an actual cut). WebFlash one-click import stays a separate, controlled follow-up (gated by the fan-token guardrail + WebFlash warning UX); the preview ARTIFACT is releasable now. No target is promoted to stable, marked recommended/default/REQUIRED_CONFIG, or allowed to claim hardware / bench / compliance / verified-schematic evidence. RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-BUILD-ROWS-001 has since added the reviewed config/webflash-builds.json PREVIEW build rows for the three compile-validated webflash-lane candidates (Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-RoomIQ, Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ, Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED), each addressed via its products/webflash wrapper on the preview (non-stable) channel with hosted-compile evidence (run 26821900127), and flipped their config/product-catalog.json rows blocked->preview. Those three targets move from publication_status eligible-unpublished to webflash-preview-metadata-ready (the WebFlash release-eligibility row now exists), their build_blocker for 'needs a reviewed config/webflash-builds.json row' is resolved (build_blocker null), and their consuming candidate bundles stay hidden / not buyable. No firmware binary, GitHub Release, tag, manifest.json, or firmware/sources.json is published; nothing is promoted to stable.
description: Concrete, CI-consumable preview / advanced-preview release-target manifest for every buildable Sense360 firmware target. Makes the eligibility policy in config/release-channel-policy.json (RELEASE-PREVIEW-ALL-PRODUCTS-001) actionable by recording, per target, the config string, buildable YAML path, channel tier + build channel, expected artifact name, the release-note warning text, WebFlash import eligibility, the delivery lane (webflash / manual-preview / advanced-manual-preview), the stable blocker, and any build blocker that prevents cutting a preview artifact now. This file is target metadata only: it publishes no firmware, adds no config/webflash-builds.json row, flips no config/product-catalog.json status, promotes nothing to stable, claims no bench evidence / compliance / hardware or build proof, and does not touch the WebFlash repo. config/webflash-builds.json remains the sole WebFlash release-eligibility source of truth; the manual-preview / advanced-manual-preview lanes are non-WebFlash preview delivery lanes for the fan / TRIAC targets. Read by tests/test_preview_release_targets.py and scripts/validate_preview_release_targets.py.

scope.notes: Concrete target metadata only; this manifest itself claims no build proof. The hosted Preview Compile Dry-Run (run 26821900127, workflow_dispatch / compile_mode=full, 2026-06-02, ESPHome 2026.4.5) has since recorded a GREEN firmware-build compile for the in-scope preview / manual-preview targets; that result lives as compile_validation_status in config/compile-only-targets.json, not here. Every target that cannot be cut still records an explicit build_blocker. Cutting an actual config/webflash-builds.json row remains a separate, proof-bearing, per-target step. RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-WRAPPERS-001 has since authored the products/webflash wrapper YAMLs for the three compile-validated webflash-lane preview candidates (Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-RoomIQ, Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ, Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED) and records each one in the per-target webflash_wrapper field; that wrappers-only change adds NO config/webflash-builds.json row, flips NO catalog status, and keeps WebFlash import/publish gated for a later reviewed build-row PR. UPDATE (RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-BUILD-ROWS-001): the reviewed config/webflash-builds.json preview build rows for Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-RoomIQ, Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ, and Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED have now been cut by that sibling PR (in config/webflash-builds.json, not by this manifest), so those three targets are recorded here as webflash-preview-metadata-ready with build_blocker null. This manifest itself still adds NO config/webflash-builds.json row (adds_webflash_builds_entries stays false), publishes no artifact, and claims no build / hardware / bench / compliance proof; the hosted run 26821900127 result remains firmware-build proof only.

delivery_lanes.webflash: Preview / advanced-preview artifact published as a WebFlash-importable build: a config/webflash-builds.json row + products/webflash wrapper + recorded build proof, exposed behind the acknowledgement gate. config/webflash-builds.json stays the sole WebFlash release-eligibility source of truth.
delivery_lanes.manual-preview: Buildable PREVIEW artifact released to testers via the manual-preview lane (config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json). The lane produces the tester-facing preview .bin -- these are real preview RELEASE targets, not passive candidates. WebFlash preview / manual-preview IMPORT is now ELIGIBLE for these fan drivers (RELEASE-PREVIEW-FAN-WEBFLASH-ELIGIBILITY-001) as an Advanced-install-only, acknowledgement-gated preview import; the committed config/webflash-builds.json one-click import row still stays out (the fan-token guardrail keeps fan drivers off the WebFlash build matrix) and is the separately queued downstream WF-IMPORT-RELAY-001 / WF-IMPORT-PWM-001 / WF-IMPORT-DAC-001 follow-up. The preview artifact itself is releasable now; no durable GitHub Release / committed WebFlash row is cut in this PR.
delivery_lanes.advanced-manual-preview: Advanced (mains-risk) PREVIEW artifact lane for TRIAC. Same as manual-preview but requires the advanced mains-risk warning copy and competent-person manual install. Releasable as advanced-preview once the target is buildable end-to-end; WebFlash import stays gated behind the advanced acknowledgement UX (separate follow-up). NOT a 'blocked-from-preview' lane: a target here is preview-allowed in principle and only an explicit build_blocker (e.g. HW-005 buildability) can stop an actual cut.

warning_copy.preview: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
warning_copy.advanced-preview: ADVANCED PREVIEW — MAINS-VOLTAGE RISK. This firmware drives mains-voltage hardware (e.g. TRIAC phase-control dimming) and is for competent persons performing a manual installation only. It is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and is NEVER a default. No bench evidence and no electrical-safety / compliance certification is claimed. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death. Do not install unless you are qualified to work on mains wiring.

targets.notes: Promoted to stable v1.0.6 (GitHub Release v1.0.6, 2026-06-09) under owner risk-acceptance waiver HW-AIRIQ-WAIVER-2026-06: AirIQ sensor-stack bench accuracy (SPS30, SGP41, SCD41, BMP390) is NOT verified and the S360-410 PoE PSU is covered by HW-S360-410-WAIVER-2026-06. The catalog entry is status production / channel stable and the config/webflash-builds.json row is channel stable, artifact Sense360-Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-RoomIQ-v1.0.6-stable.bin. The S360-KIT-KITCHEN-P bundle stays hidden / not buyable / not recommended / not a customer default. targets.notes: One buildable firmware (Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED) consumed by both the Living-room and Corridor room bundles, and DISTINCT from the published VentIQ LED preview (Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-RoomIQ-LED), which is unchanged. RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-BUILD-ROWS-001 added the reviewed config/webflash-builds.json preview build row (product_yaml products/webflash/ceiling-poe-roomiq-led.yaml, channel preview, artifact Sense360-Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED-v1.0.0-preview.bin) with hosted-compile evidence (run 26821900127) and flipped the config/product-catalog.json entry from blocked to preview (webflash_build_matrix true; target_channel preview-candidate, LED stays preview). publication_status is now webflash-preview-metadata-ready: the WebFlash release-eligibility row exists, but NO firmware binary / GitHub Release / tag / manifest.json / firmware/sources.json is published, and the S360-KIT-LIVING-P / S360-KIT-CORRIDOR-P candidate bundles stay hidden / not buyable. Not recommended, not customer-default, not stable; LED stays preview and stable stays gated by S360-410 (PRODUCT-POE-410-001) + the LED preview-to-stable gauntlet. The products/webflash wrapper (RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-WRAPPERS-001) is recorded in webflash_wrapper; the compile dry-run is recorded GREEN by hosted run 26821900127 (validated-full-compile), firmware-build proof only.

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: Preview / manual-preview WebFlash import is ELIGIBLE (RELEASE-PREVIEW-FAN-WEBFLASH-ELIGIBILITY-001 removes the upstream import block): WebFlash may import this fan-driver preview as an Advanced-install-only, acknowledgement-gated preview build -- missing hardware / bench / compliance evidence blocks stable / full release only, never the preview import. Eligibility is NOT a committed build row: no fan row is added to config/webflash-builds.json (the fan-token guardrail in scripts/list_release_targets.py and the catalog webflash_build_matrix=false both stand), so the preview artifact stays on the manual-preview lane (config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json) and the actual WebFlash-side one-click committed import is the separately queued downstream follow-up (WEBFLASH-RELAY-001 / WEBFLASH-PWM-001 / WEBFLASH-DAC-001, imported by WF-IMPORT-RELAY-001 / WF-IMPORT-PWM-001 / WF-IMPORT-DAC-001). Not stable, not recommended, not a default, not buyable.

webflash_import_eligibility.reason: Mains-voltage TRIAC phase control. Advanced-manual-preview lane: the advanced (mains-risk) preview artifact is delivered via the advanced-manual-preview lane. The HW-005 buildability blocker is resolved (TRIAC-UNBLOCK-BUILD-001; SX1509-free Core respin routes TRI_GPIO1/TRI_GPIO2 direct to IO14/IO13; local full compile recorded), so the target is buildable; publishing the artifact is the follow-up TRIAC-PUBLISH-ADVANCED-PREVIEW-001. WebFlash one-click import stays gated behind the advanced acknowledgement UX (separate follow-up: WF-IMPORT-TRIAC-001) and is not enabled here. Advanced-preview ceiling: never stable, never recommended, never a default, never a REQUIRED_CONFIG, never a customer-kit default.

targets.release_note_warning: ADVANCED PREVIEW — MAINS-VOLTAGE RISK. This firmware drives mains-voltage hardware (e.g. TRIAC phase-control dimming) and is for competent persons performing a manual installation only. It is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and is NEVER a default. No bench evidence and no electrical-safety / compliance certification is claimed. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death. Do not install unless you are qualified to work on mains wiring.
targets.stable_blocker: PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 (operator bench protocol with signed attestation) + experimental-lane preconditions per COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001 (COMPLIANCE-001 CLOSED by market posture — never placed on the market; see docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md).
targets.notes: Mains-voltage TRIAC. Advanced-preview ONLY, with mandatory mains-risk warning copy, delivered via the advanced-manual-preview lane. TRIAC-UNBLOCK-BUILD-001 resolved the HW-005 BUILDABILITY blocker (SX1509-free Core respin routes TRI_GPIO1/TRI_GPIO2 direct to IO14/IO13; build_blocker cleared; local full compile recorded in config/preview-fan-triac-build-rows.json). The artifact is buildable but is NOT published here: publishing the advanced-manual-preview .bin is the follow-up TRIAC-PUBLISH-ADVANCED-PREVIEW-001, now owned by the commissioning PR under COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001 (COMPLIANCE-001 closed by posture; publish gated by PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 with signed attestation + the experimental-lane preconditions). Never stable, recommended, default, REQUIRED_CONFIG, or WebFlash-exposed in this PR.
